title: Odes Of Anacreon - Ode LXXVI.
content:
Hither, gentle Muse of mine,
        Come and teach thy votary old
    Many a golden hymn divine,
        For the nymph with vest of gold.

    Pretty nymph, of tender age,
        Fair thy silky looks unfold;
    Listen to a hoary sage,
        Sweetest maid with vest of gold!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Thomas Moore delivers a powerful performance in "Odes Of Anacreon - Ode LXXVI."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
